title: Petri / Colored Petri Net (CPN)
description: How you-agent-factory maps factory topology onto a Petri / Colored Petri Net (CPN) execution model: places, colored tokens, transitions, and markings.
openingSummary: Petri / Colored Petri Net (CPN) framing is how you-agent-factory reads factory topology as a net: work tokens move through places under workstation transitions.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: This page explains Petri / Colored Petri Net (CPN) framing for you-agent-factory. It covers how authored factory topology becomes a net the runtime executes, and how places, tokens, transitions, and markings map onto factory nouns.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: In factory terms, a place is a work-type state such as task:init. A colored token is submitted work occupying a place, carrying work-type identity and payload. A transition is a workstation that consumes inputs and emits outcomes. A marking is which tokens currently sit in which places.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Read a factory run as a Petri / CPN: work enters an initial place, a workstation becomes enabled when its input places have matching tokens, the transition fires and dispatches a worker, and the token routes to an accepted, continue, rejection, or failure place.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is Petri / CPN framing for factory readers. It is not academic Petri-net theory, not the JavaScript dynamic-workflow orchestrator deep dive, not the configuration field reference, and not the architecture-of-system diagram page.
